findings.hypothesis_text: CD8+ T cell density varies significantly across spatial tumor regions (Tumor Edge, Stroma, Tumor Islet) in high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma.
findings.effect: Tumor Edge showed the highest mean CD8+ deconvolution fraction (0.005-0.040), followed by Stroma (0.007-0.032), then Tumor Islet (0.002-0.021), consistent across all 7 patients; Tumor Edge vs. Tumor Islet trended (adj. p=0.052) but no pairwise contrast reached significance after Bonferroni correction.
findings.reason: Small cohort size (n=7 patients) resulted in all pairwise Wilcoxon comparisons (Tumor Edge vs. Tumor Islet, Stroma vs. Tumor Islet, Tumor Edge vs. Stroma) failing to reach significance after Bonferroni correction (all adj. p > 0.05), despite a consistent directional trend across patients.
findings.provenance: MOSAIC-OV Visium spatial transcriptomics, n=7 HGSOC patients; CD8+ deconvolution fraction (t_cd8_deconv_sample_level_custom1_frac) compared across annotated regions via Wilcoxon paired tests with Bonferroni adjustment.
findings.author: Yousuf
findings.markdown:
**[INTERNAL]**
### CD8+ T cell density gradient across tumor regions in HGSOC (underpowered)
- **Disease**: HGSOC
- **Status**: abandoned
- **Effect**: Tumor Edge (0.005-0.040) > Stroma (0.007-0.032) > Tumor Islet (0.002-0.021); Tumor Edge vs. Tumor Islet adj. p=0.052, all other pairs n.s. after Bonferroni
- **Entities**: CD8A, Tumor_Edge, Stroma, Tumor_Islet
- **Reason**: n=7 cohort — no pairwise contrast reached significance after Bonferroni correction, despite a consistent directional trend across all patients

A consistent Tumor Edge > Stroma > Tumor Islet gradient was observed across all 7 patients, biologically consistent with immune exclusion (CD8+ enriched at the invasive front, depleted within tumor nests). Do NOT report as a confirmed significant result without expanding the cohort or switching to an exploratory/FDR framing explicitly.
